ground_truth.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 45 Deals Announced in October 2025
ground_truth.author: Eduard Kovacs
ground_truth.main_content: Forty-five cybersecurity merger and acquisition (M&A) deals were announced in October 2025. Out of the 45 deals, roughly a quarter had publicly reported financial terms. A majority of deals were smaller, highly strategic acquisitions focusing on acquiring high-value tech or talent. Private equity firms were active in both large-scale buyouts and consolidation plays, showing confidence in GRC and other mature platforms. In fact, GRC companies were involved in over a dozen deals. The data shows an effort to consolidate GRC platforms, particularly through the integration of niche expertise such as third-party risk management and AI governance. Half a dozen deals involved identity and authentication firms, with several major companies looking to add next-generation features such as passwordless authentication and identity threat detection and response into their core identity platforms. While a majority of the target companies are based in the United States, a significant number of deals involve Australian companies, with several transactions pointing to consolidation within the local market. Key deals include: Dataminr acquires ThreatConnect for $290 million; Imprivata acquires Verosint; Francisco Partners to acquire Jamf for $2.2 billion; JumpCloud acquires Breez; LevelBlue acquires Cybereason; NSO Group acquired by US investors; Pentera acquires DevOcean; Ping Identity to acquire Keyless; RecordPoint acquires Redactive; Riveron acquires Eden Data; Searchlight Cyber acquires Intangic; Twilio to acquire Stytch; Vectra AI acquires Netography; Veeam Software to acquire Securiti AI for $1.725 billion.
with: Forty-five cybersecurity merger and acquisition (M&A) deals were announced in October 2025.
GRC companies were involved in over a dozen deals, showing an effort to consolidate GRC platforms.
Half a dozen deals involved identity and authentication firms, with companies adding passwordless authentication and identity threat detection.
Dataminr is acquiring threat intelligence firm ThreatConnect for $290 million in cash and equity.
Veeam Software has announced plans to acquire data security posture management company Securiti AI for $1.725 billion.
